programme.title: Loophole
programme.desc: Unlikely partners Mike Daniels, an expert safe cracker and failed architect Stephen Booker prepare to extract a fortune in cash and jewels from the vault of one of London's largest holdings banks. After months of painstaking planning it seems their plan cannot fail. Disaster however can come in the strangest of places and an unexpected thunderstorm alters both of their plans.

programme.title: Ghoomer
programme.desc: Batting prodigy Anina loses her hand on the eve of her international cricketing debut. A failed cricketer enters her life and with innovative training, she becomes a bowler.

programme.title: Manon Des Sources
programme.desc: The stunning sequel to Jean de Florette. Manon, a beautiful young shepherdess living in the idyllic Provencal countryside, discovers the truth surrounding her father's death and decides to take revenge upon the men responsible.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: Some of the biggest film stars in history rose to fame during the 'golden age' of Hollywood, in the early and mid-20th century. We profile some of these stars who became household names.

programme.title: Le Samourai
programme.desc: After carrying out a flawlessly planned hit, Jef Costello, a contract killer with samurai instincts, finds himself caught between a persistent police investigator and a ruthless employer, and not even his armor of fedora and trench coat can protect him.

programme.title: Lassie: A New Adventure
programme.desc: The most famous Collie dog Lassie is on a rescue mission with her best friend Flo and their new friends Henri, Kleo and little Pippa, a Jack Russell Terrier. This time round, Lassie's detective skills are particularly called upon to get to the bottom of the mysterious disappearances of several pedigree dogs.

programme.title: The Three Musketeers Part I: D'Artagnan
programme.desc: Young d'Artagnan arrives in Paris and joins the Musketeers. They must defend the crown against a conspiracy threatening the kingdom.

programme.title: Heavy Trip
programme.desc: A young man is trying to overcome his fears by leading the most unknown heavy metal band in Finland, Impaled Rektum, to the hottest metal festival of Norway.

programme.title: Song to Song
programme.desc: Song to Song is a love story set in Austin's rock scene, following four lovers navigating music careers and tumultuous relationships. Directed by Terrence Malick, it explores passion, creativity, and modern love.

programme.title: Snatch
programme.desc: Unscrupulous boxing promoters, violent bookmakers, a Russian gangster, incompetent amateur robbers and jewellers fight amongst themselves to track down a priceless stolen diamond. Directed by Guy Ritchie and features and all-star cast, including Brad Pitt, Jason Statham, Benicio Del Toro and Stephen Graham.

programme.title: Beneath Hill 60
programme.desc: By 1916, the Great War in Europe has reached a stalemate. The two massive armies have fought themselves to a standstill. Millions of lives have already been lost. Mining engineer Oliver Woodward returns to Australia from Papua to join a secret Australian Tunnelling Company. Ordinary miners from all over the country are given just two weeks' military training before being sent to the Western Front. Their task is to undermine the German lines.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: David Stratton interviews Edouard Niermans and Fanny Bastien about Angel Dust. Margaret Pomeranz talks to Bryan Brown and producer Ben Gannon about the making of Confidence.

programme.title: A United Kingdom
programme.desc: The story of Oxford-educated King Seretse Khama of Botswana and how his loving but controversial marriage to white Englishwoman Ruth Williams in 1948 plunged his kingdom into political and diplomatic turmoil.

programme.title: Lassie: A New Adventure
programme.desc: The most famous Collie dog Lassie is on a rescue mission with her best friend Flo and their new friends Henri, Kleo and little Pippa, a Jack Russell Terrier. This time round, Lassie's detective skills are particularly called upon to get to the bottom of the mysterious disappearances of several pedigree dogs.

programme.title: White Squall
programme.desc: A true story about a group of troubled American teenage boys in the 1960s, who were taken to crew a school sailing ship for eight months. Their friendship is put to the test during a freak storm.

programme.title: Let Me Go
programme.desc: A neighbour takes care of Claudine's son while she goes to a mountain hotel to meet men passing through. When one of them decides to extend his stay, Claudine finds herself dreaming of another life.

programme.title: Viceroy's House
programme.desc: In 1947, Lord Mountbatten assumes the post of last Viceroy, charged with handing India back to its people, living upstairs at the house which was the home of British rulers. (Hugh Bonneville).

programme.title: Loophole
programme.desc: When architect Stephen Booker loses his partnership, he finds jobs hard to come by, and with money in short supply, he unwittingly becomes involved in a daring scheme to rob one of London's biggest bank vaults. Starring Martin Sheen.

programme.title: The Amazing Mr. Blunden
programme.desc: Complex, Dickensian ghost story about two young children who move with their mother to an old mansion. The genial resident ghost introduces them to two ghost children, who ask them to solve an ancient mystery. This requires them to travel back in time to save two orphans from a dreadful uncle and a terrible death in a burning house.

programme.title: The King Of Laughter
programme.desc: Naples, 1904: Italian theatre is thriving, and comic actor, Eduardo Scarpetta is the box office king. Known in the Neapolitan theater for his cheeky alter egos, Scarpetta’s larger-than-life stage productions were matched only by his eccentric personal life. Composed of wives, partners, lovers, legitimate and illegitimate children (including a young Eduardo De Filippo, famed Neapolitan playwright), Scarpetta’s home situation resembled one of his comedies more than a traditional family. At the height of his popularity, the comedian risked everything by staging a parody of the great Italian poet, Gabriele D’Annunzio. Booed and hissed from the stage by younger rivals, Scarpetta was sued for plagiarism, leading to the first ever copyright lawsuit in Italy. Despite legal troubles and familial strife, Scarpetta fought not only for his craft but for his legacy as one of the great thespians of Italian theater.

programme.title: Fame
programme.desc: Young men and women audition for coveted spots at the New York High School of Performing Arts. Those who make the cut discover that it takes a lot of hard work to become a star and sometimes difficult decisions have to be made. The youngsters grapple with heavy issues such as homosexuality, abortion, attempted suicide and illiteracy. On top of their unique struggles, the students must deal with the mundane pressures of adolescence like homework, heartbreak and rejection.

programme.title: Made In America
programme.desc: Grown-up comedy. When Nia takes a blood test at school, she finds her mum (Whoopi Goldberg) has deceived her and that her father is a politically incorrect car salesman (Ted Danson).

programme.title: The Three Musketeers Part I: D'Artagnan
programme.desc: Constance Bonacieux is kidnapped before D'Artagnan's very eyes. In a frantic quest to save her, the young musketeer, aided by Athos, Porthos and Aramis, is forced to join forces with the mysterious Milady de Winter.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Enjoy these classic episodes of The Movie Show with living legends, Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton, as they review the latest crop of movies and give us their rating out of 5-stars.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz interviews Terry Gilliam about Baron, his goals and actors Robin Williams and Oliver Reed. David Stratton talks to Anne Turner about Celia and its two connected story strands.

programme.title: The Most Beautiful Day In The World
programme.desc: A theatre entrepreneur has no talent for art or business, but when a distant relative entrusts him with the care of his nephew, the man discovers he has a phenomenal talent that could change the fate of his business.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: In this episode, Margaret Pomeranz talks to Tian Zhuang Zhuang about Horse Thief, its violence and film themes. David Stratton interviews Tait Brady and Paul Byrnes on film festivals and featured films.

programme.title: The Amazing Mr. Blunden
programme.desc: Complex, Dickensian ghost story about two young children who move with their mother to an old mansion. The genial resident ghost introduces them to two ghost children, who ask them to solve an ancient mystery. This requires them to travel back in time to save two orphans from a dreadful uncle and a terrible death in a burning house.

programme.title: The Three Musketeers Part I: D'Artagnan
programme.desc: Constance Bonacieux is kidnapped before D'Artagnan's very eyes. In a frantic quest to save her, the young musketeer, aided by Athos, Porthos and Aramis, is forced to join forces with the mysterious Milady de Winter.

programme.title: Fame
programme.desc: Young men and women audition for coveted spots at the New York High School of Performing Arts. Those who make the cut discover that it takes a lot of hard work to become a star and sometimes difficult decisions have to be made. The youngsters grapple with heavy issues such as homosexuality, abortion, attempted suicide and illiteracy. On top of their unique struggles, the students must deal with the mundane pressures of adolescence like homework, heartbreak and rejection.
